publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-02-08 23:23:19 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Department of Romance Studies

did.abstract: Records relating to the management of the journal. Includes administrative files, proofs and galleys, issues of Diacritics, submissions and reviews of articles. Also interviews with individuals involved in the publication.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Diacritics is a forum for scholars writing on the problems of literary criticism, based in the Dept. of Romance Studies at Cornell University, published by The Johns Hopkins University Press. It was founded in 1971 under the editorship of David Grossvogel.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Records relating to the management of the journal. Includes administrative files, proofs and galleys, issues of Diacritics, submissions and reviews of articles. Also interviews with individuals involved in the publication.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series III. Submissions and Reviews Boxes 6-14
Series IV. Issues of Diacritics Boxes 15-16

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Diacritics Collection, #6988. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restriced to the permission of the editor of Diacritics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Invitations to join and requests to resign from the Diacritics editorial board

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: internal correspondence pertaining to the Diacritics editorial board, often calls for meetings and descriptions of previous meetings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: internal correspondence from Diacritics editor Debra Castillo. Extensive and rich documentation of her (and assistant editor Kate Bloodgood's) attempts to reform the editorial processes of the Diacritics editorial board.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: internal correspondence from Diacritics editor Richard Klein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editor Joan Ramon Resina and other members of Diacritics editorial board and staff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from outgoing editor Philip Lewis to new editor Debra Castillo to facilitate transition.

did.unittitle: D. I. Grossvogel to unknown (Diacritics editor?) regarding metacriticism

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Diacritics and Johns Hopkins University Press regarding marketing. Several documents regarding advertisement exchanges with other journals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Diacritics and Johns Hopkins University Press regarding the journal's finances.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Administrative and other assorted correspondence between Johns Hopkins University Press and Diacritics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editor Debra Castillo and outside scholars. Many solicitations for review articles, notably from Rodolphe Gasché and Anthony Appiah.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editor Bruno Bosteels and outside scholars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editor Joan Ramon Resina and outside scholars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editors Philip Lewis, Debra Castillo, Richard Klein, outside scholars, and staff and board members of Diacritics on proposals for review articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editor Joan Ramon Resina, outside scholars, and staff and board members of Diacritics on proposals for review articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editor Philip Lewis and outside scholars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editor Philip Lewis and outside scholars, especially pertaining to revising submissions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between editor Jonathan Culler and outside scholars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposals for review articles from outside scholars, often with extensive notes and/or back and forth correspondence with editor Debra Castillo and assistant editor Kate Bloodgood.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various financial records from twenty years of the journal's history.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Details of tasks and hiring processes of editorial assistant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inquiries and responses to permissions requests

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Legal documents pertaining to the founding of Diacritics. Also includes the minutes of the first Diacritics board meeting in 1971.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inventories of articles published in Diacritics from 1987-2009.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on style requirements for Diacritics submissions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special issue: "Around the Work of Benedict Anderson"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additionally contains some correspondence and examples of art used in the issue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Earlier stage in editing process than other file of same name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Earlier stage in editing process than other file of same name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special issue: "Essays in Memory of Pierre Klossowski"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Later in the design process than other file of same name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special issue "Literature and the Right to Marriage"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special issue: "The Thought of Roberto Esposito"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special issue: "Taking Exception to the Exception"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs of Diacritics issue 25.1 featuring illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Design notes for original format of Diacritics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Guidelines for proposals for Diacritics special issues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Anthony Appiah and other intellectuals on possibility of a Diacritics issue devoted to African American thought and letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for special issue of Diacritics by Philip Lewis and Georges Van Den Abbeele devoted to Descartes.

did.unittitle: Special issue proposal: Philippe Lacoue-Labarthe and Jean-Luc Nancy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal by Peter Fenves and Susan Berstein for a special issue of Diacritics issue devoted to the thought of Philippe Lacoue-Labarthe and Jean-Luc Nancy.

did.unittitle: Special issue proposal: Views of America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for special issue of Diacritics by Jose Piedra on America.

did.unittitle: Special issue proposal: Latin American Literature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for special issue of Diacritics by Debra Castillo devoted to Latin-American Literature. Ultimately published as Diacritics 25.1, "Latin/American Issues"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Internal and external correspondence planning the twenty-fifth anniversary issue of Diacritics, involving notes from board meetings, internal correspondence, and external solicitation of contributions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft and notes from Richard Klein's "Nuclear Criticism," an article that appeared in Yale French Studies but that can be read as a corollary to the special issue of Diacritics of the same name, 14.2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal from Jonathan Monroe for special issue of Diacritics: "Poetry, Community, and Movement." Ultimately published as Diacritics 26.3-4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal from Nelly Furman for special issue of Diacritics devoted to Georges Bataille. Published as Diacritics 26.2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal by Pheng Cheah and Elizabeth Grosz for special issue of Diacritics devoted to Luce Irigaray. Ultimately published as Diacritics 28.1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for special issue of Diacritics by Jonathan Strauss on Death after the French Revolution. Ultimately published as Diacritics 30.3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for special issue of Diacritics by Biddy Martin and Richard Juang on transgender theory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for special issue of Diacritics by Jonathan Culler and Pheng Cheah on Benedict Anderson. Ultimately published as Diacritics 29.4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for colloquium on Jacques Derrida's Glas, on its 25th anniversary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal by Simon Jarvis for a special issue of Diacritics devoted to rethinking beauty. Eventually published as Diacritics 32.1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal by Steven Miller for a special issue of Diacritics devoted to Marriage. Eventually published as Diacritics 35.4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal by Simone Pinet and Oscar Martin for a special issue of Diacritics devoted to Medieval Iberia. Published as Diacritics 36.3-4

did.unittitle: Special issue proposal: Laruelle and Nonphilosophy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for a special issue of Diacritics devoted to François Laruelle and Nonphilosophy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for a special issue by Namita Goswami of Diacritics devoted to Edward Said

did.unittitle: Special issue proposal: Biopolitics and Roberto Esposito

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal from Timothy Murray for special issue of Diacritics devoted to biopolitics and the thought of Roberto Esposito, which would ultimately appear as Diacritics 36.2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal from Bruno Bosteels for special issue of Diacritics devoted to Jacques Rancière and Politics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal from Jonathan Culler and Mark Seltzer for special issue of Diacritics devoted to science.

did.unittitle: Trevor Hope, "Sexual Indifference and the Homosexual Male Imaginary"
Elizabeth A. Povinelli, "Sexual Savages/Sexual Sovereignty: Australian Colonial Texts and the Postcolonial Politics of Nationalism"
James Slawney, "Hallucinogenic Literature: Avital Ronell's Narcoanalysis"
Jacques Derrida, "Archive Fever: A Freudian Impression"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Highly significant piece by Jacques Derrida originally published in English in Diacritics 25.2. Presented here with correspondence regarding translation and the securing of rights.

did.unittitle: Gordon C.F. Bearn, "Derrida Dry: Iterating Iterability Analytically"
Jill Robbins, "Circumcising Confession: Derrida, Autobiography, Judaism"
Steven Bretzius, "Dr. Jacques L. and Martin Hide-a-Guerre: The Subject of New Historicism"
Jonathan Loesberg, "Materialism and Aesthetics: Paul de Man's 'Aesthetic Ideology'"
Tom Conley, "Putting French Studies on the Map"
Françoise Lionnet, "Reframing Baudelaire: Literary History, Biography, Postcolonial Theory, and Vernacular Languages"
Richard Burt and Jeffrey Wallen, "Knowing Better: Sex, Cultural Criticism, and the Pedagogical Imperatives of the 1990s"
Patrick Paul Garlinger, "Homo-ness and the Fear of Femininity"
Amanda Anderson, "Realism, Universalism, and the Science of the Human"
Carl Good, "Don Quijote and the Law of Literature"
E.C. Graf, "When an Arab Laughs in Toledo: Cervantes's Interpellation of Early Modern Spanish Orientalism"
David Kellogg, "Perloff's Wittgenstein: W(h)ither Poetic Theory?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft titled "Avoiding the Subject: Marjorie Perloff and the Neutralization of Theory" ultimately published in Diacritics 26.3-4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Evaluation file on accepted article ultimately not published in Diacritics.

did.unittitle: Claudia Breger, "The Leader's Two Bodies: Slavoj Žižek's Postmodern Political Theology"
Mark Hansen, "Seeing with the Body: The Digital Image in Postphotography"
Ming Dong Gu, "The Universal "One": Toward a Common Conceptual Basis for Chinese and Western Studies"
Stefano Franchi, "Hunters, Cooks, and Nooks: Two Interpretations of the Tangled Relationship between Philosophy and Science"
Simon Critchley and Tom McCarthy, "Universal Shylockery"
Leland de la Durantaye, "The Suspended Substantive: On Animals and Men in Giorgio Agamben's The Open"
Kenneth Reinhard and Julia Reinhard Lupton, "The Subject of Religion: Lacan and the Ten Commandments"
Anne-Lise François, "'O Happy Living Things': Frankenfoods and the Bounds of Wordsworthian Natural Piety"
Alexander E. Hooke, "Alphonso Lingis's We--A Collage, Not a Collective"
Malcolm K. Read, "Benítez Rojo and Las Casas's Plague of Ants: The Libidinal Versus the Ideological Unconscious"
James Penney, "(Queer) Theory and the Universal Alternative"
Tim Dean, "Art as Symptom: Žižek and the Ethics of Psychoanalytic Criticism"
Ernesto Laclau, "Can Immanence Explain Social Struggles?"
Judith Butler, "Giving an Account of Oneself"
Orrin Wang, "Against Theory Beside Romanticism: The Sensation of the Signifier"
Anjali Prabhu, "Interrogating Hybridity: Subaltern Agency and Totality in Postcolonial Theory"
Anthony Sampson, "Freud on the State, Violence, and War"
Michèle H. Richman, "Spitting Images in Montaigne and Bataille: For a Heterological Counterhistory of Sovereignty"
Michael F. Bernard-Donals, "'Difficult Freedom': Levinas, Language, and Politics"
Oliver Marchart, "In the Name of the People: Populist Reason and the Subject of the Political"
Mikko Tuhkanen, "Of Blackface and Paranoid Knowledge: Richard Wright, Jacques Lacan, and the Ambivalence of Black Minstrelsy"
Charles Shepherdson, "A Pound of Flesh: Lacan's Reading of 'The Visible and the Invisible'"
Elizabeth Maddock Dillon, "Fear of Formalism: Kant, Twain, and Cultural Studies in American Literature"
Ignacio Javier López, "Film, Freud, and Paranoia: Dali and the Representation of Male Desire in 'An Andalusian Dog'"
Amit Pinchevski, "Freedom from Speech (Or the Silent Demand)"
Joseph Suglia, "The Communication of the Impossible"
Brett Levinson, "Sex Without Sex, Queering the Market, the Collapse of the Political, the Death of Difference, and Aids: Hailing Judith Butler"
Bryan Reynolds and Joseph Fitzpatrick, "The Transversality of Michel de Certeau: Foucault's Panoptic Discourse and the Cartographic Impulse"
Charles Stewart, "Syncretism and Its Synonyms: Reflections on Cultural Mixture"
Neill Matheson, "Identifying (with) the Queerness of Melville's 'Pierre'"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers by Jonathan Culler, Judith Butler, Lee Edelman, and Jane Gallop ultimately published in Diacritics 34.1

did.unittitle: Stewart Martin, "Adorno's Conception of the Form of Philosophy"
Bruno Chaouat, "In the Image of Auschwitz"
Carrie Noland, "Red Front/Black Front: Aimé Césaire and the Affaire Aragon"
Kirk Wetters, "The Rule of the Norm and the Political Theology of 'Real life' in Carl Schmitt and Giorgio Agamben"
L. Scott Lerner, "Mourning and Subjectivity: From Bersani to Proust, Klein, and Freud"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These papers are filed in mainly chronological order within the boxes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple submissions on Heidegger, Butler, Deleuze, Derrida, Said, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple submissions on Said, de Man, Irigaray, Derida, Žižek, Kant, Nancy, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple submissions on Derrida, Deleuze, Wittgenstein, Kant, etc.

did.unittitle: Interview with Philip Lewis with Laurent Ferri and Diane Brown
